paper_title: Photo-Fenton Catalytic Activity and Degradation Pathway Analysis

methods.description: Measurement of sulfamethoxazole (SMX) degradation efficiency using photo-Fenton catalysts under visible light irradiation, varying parameters such as catalyst calcination temperature, H2O2 concentration, solution pH, and SMX loading.

steps.description: the SMX adsorption by the catalysts became optimal after 30 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate catalyst with SMX solution for adsorption.

steps.description: After this time, the SMX-sorbed catalysts were irradiated using visible light for 120 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform photo-Fenton reaction under visible light irradiation.

steps.description: The efficiency of SMX degradation by the four photo-Fenton catalysts was measured as a function of the UIO-66/DE calcination temperature, initial concentration of H2O2, solution pH, and SMX loading.
measured as a function of the UIO-66/DE calcination temperature, initial concentration of H2O2, solution pH, and SMX loading.
steps.searchable_summary: Vary experimental parameters (catalyst type/calcination, H2O2 concentration, pH, SMX loading) and repeat measurements.
outputs: SMX degradation efficiency data as a function of varied parameters

methods.description: Measurement of electrochemical impedance to probe electron transfer properties of the catalysts.

steps.description: The electrochemical impedance spectroscopic (EIS) measurements were carried out to probe the electron transfer between the electrode surface and the solution interface.

methods.description: Construction of Tafel polarization plots to assess catalyst corrosion current and stability.

steps.description: The Tafel polarization plots were constructed using the materials received under calcination at different temperatures in Figure S4b.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Tafel polarization measurements and construct plots.

methods.description: Measurement of the photocurrent generated by catalysts under irradiation to assess charge separation efficiency.

steps.description: A xenon lamp was used to irradiate the catalyst.
steps.searchable_summary: Irradiate the catalyst sample using a xenon lamp.

steps.description: Figure 4d shows the photocurrent responses of the catalysts fabricated at different calcination temperatures.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure the photocurrent response of the catalyst under illumination.

methods.description: Evaluation of catalyst stability and reusability through multiple cycles of the photo-Fenton degradation process, including washing and drying steps.

steps.description: For these experiments, 0.40 g of catalyst, 20 mg/L SMX, and 4 mM H2O2 were used in a pH 3 solution.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform one cycle of SMX degradation under specified conditions.

steps.description: After each cycle, the used catalyst was washed three times with deionized water
steps.searchable_summary: Wash the used catalyst with deionized water.
Collect the washed catalyst using centrifugation.

steps.description: The material was dried at 80 • C to commence the next cycle.
The efficiency of SMX degradation is shown in Figure 5b.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure the SMX degradation efficiency for the completed cycle.
inputs: Degraded SMX solution from the cycle
outputs: SMX degradation efficiency (%) for the cycle

steps.description: This process was repeated five times.
steps.searchable_summary: Repeat steps 1-5 for a specified number of cycles using the recovered catalyst.
outputs: SMX degradation efficiency data over multiple cycles

methods.method_name: SMX Degradation Intermediate Analysis and Pathway Proposal
methods.description: Identification of intermediate products formed during SMX degradation and proposal of possible degradation pathways based on experimental data (mass spectrometry, TOC) and theoretical calculations (Fukui function, electron density).

steps.description: SMX can be mineralized into smaller molecules in the HO-UIO-66/DE-300/H2O2/light system.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform SMX degradation experiment to generate intermediates.

steps.description: The mass spectra of the SMX intermediates are shown in Figure S7
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze the degraded solution using mass spectrometry to obtain mass spectra of intermediates.

steps.description: in combination with the calculation of the reactive sites on SMX via the Fukui function (Figure 7a) and the electron density distribution of SMX (Figure 7b).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform theoretical calculations (Fukui function, electron density distribution) on the SMX molecule.

steps.description: Five possible degradation pathways were proposed based on the degradation of SMX and the detected intermediates (Figure 7e and Table S2).
steps.searchable_summary: Propose possible SMX degradation pathways based on identified intermediates and theoretical calculations.
outputs: Proposed degradation pathways (schematic diagrams, reaction steps)

steps.description: after 5 cycles, approximately 51.1% of the TOC of SMX solution was degraded
steps.searchable_summary: Measure the Total Organic Carbon (TOC) degradation of the SMX solution.
